data.title: Globus Medical Executive Sells 7,500 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameHuller Kelly
RelationshipSVP, GC, Corporate Secretary, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$675,000
SEC FilingForm 4

Kelly Huller, SVP, GC, Corporate Secretary and 10% Owner at Globus Medical, sold 7,500 shares of Class A Common Stock on November 25, 2025, at a price of $90.0 per share, totaling $675,000. Following this transaction, Huller no longer directly owns any shares of Class A Common Stock in the company. The sale was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on December 4, 2024.

SEC Filing: GLOBUS MEDICAL INC [ GMED ] - Form 4 - Nov. 28, 2025

data.title: BioLife Solutions Executives and Director Sell Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Mathew Aby J.EVP & Chief Scientific Officer, 10% OwnerSell$14,065Form 4
Foster Karen A.Chief Quality and Operations, 10% OwnerSell$11,097Form 4
Wichterman TroyChief Financial Officer, 10% OwnerSell$15,616Form 4
Duross AmyDirector, 10% OwnerSell$108,000Form 4

Several executives and a director at BioLife Solutions have recently sold shares of the company, according to SEC Form 4 filings.

Mathew Aby J., EVP & Chief Scientific Officer and 10% owner, sold 526 shares at $26.74 each, totaling $14,065. Post-transaction, Aby directly owns 350,386 shares.

Karen A. Foster, Chief Quality and Operations Officer and a 10% owner, sold 415 shares at $26.74 each, totaling $11,097. Foster now directly owns 192,838 shares.

Troy Wichterman, Chief Financial Officer and 10% owner, sold 584 shares at $26.74 each, totaling $15,616. Wichterman's direct ownership stands at 182,769 shares following the sale.

Amy DuRoss, a director and 10% owner, sold 4,000 shares at $27.00 each, totaling $108,000. DuRoss directly owns 20,687 shares after the transactions.

data.title: StoneX Group Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
StoneX Group Inc., a global financial services network, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing a year of robust financial performance, strategic acquisitions, and operational growth. The company, which operates across various financial markets and serves clients in over 180 countries, has demonstrated resilience and adaptability in a challenging economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $132,378.2 million, a 33% increase from the previous year, driven by strong performances in equity trading and prime brokerage.

Operating Revenues: $4,126.9 million, up 20% due to the acquisition of RJO and growth in listed derivatives and securities transactions.

Net Operating Revenues: $2,052.8 million, reflecting a 16% increase from the prior year, attributed to higher transaction volumes and improved revenue capture.

Net Income: $305.9 million, a 17% increase despite challenges such as diminished commodity volatility and increased interest expenses.

Diluted EPS: $5.89, compared to $5.31 in the previous year, showcasing the company's strong financial performance and effective cost management.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: StoneX operates through four main segments: Commercial, Institutional, Self-Directed/Retail, and Payments. Each segment offers a range of services, including risk management, trade execution, clearing, and advisory services, tailored to different client needs.

Geographical Performance: The company serves clients in over 180 countries, leveraging its global infrastructure and regulatory compliance to provide access to financial and physical markets worldwide. This extensive reach supports its diverse client base, including commercial entities, institutional investors, and retail clients.

Sales Units: StoneX provides access to over 18,000 global financial markets, including derivatives exchanges, foreign exchange markets, and securities exchanges. It also offers physical trading in commodities such as precious metals, energy, and agricultural products.

New Product Launches: The company continues to expand its product offerings, including customized OTC structures for commodity price and financial risk management, and a comprehensive investment banking platform that includes equity research and trading.

Future Outlook: StoneX is well-positioned to capitalize on trends such as increased regulation, consolidation in the financial services sector, and the growing importance of secure cross-border transactions. It plans to continue expanding its market share through strategic acquisitions and by targeting underserved client segments.

Operational Strategy: The company focuses on mitigating market risk, ensuring liquidity, and maintaining a variable cost structure. It employs a centralized approach to capital allocation and risk management while delegating execution to experienced managers.

Client Base Expansion: StoneX actively seeks to expand its client base through direct sales efforts, digital marketing, and strategic partnerships. It also pursues acquisitions that align with its client-centric approach and help increase market share in existing and new markets.

Strategic Initiatives

Acquisitions: StoneX completed the acquisition of R.J. O’Brien, significantly enhancing its position as a leading futures commission merchant (FCM) and expanding its client base. Additionally, the company acquired The Benchmark Company, LLC to strengthen its offerings in equity and debt capital markets, particularly enhancing its equity research and investment banking capabilities.

Capital Management: To finance the acquisition of R.J. O’Brien, StoneX issued $625 million in Senior Secured Notes due 2032, with an interest rate of 6.875% per annum. The company also completed a three-for-two stock split, distributing additional shares to shareholders to improve stock liquidity. Furthermore, StoneX increased its interest and fee income on client balances, driven by the acquisition of RJO, which contributed significantly to the company's financial results.

Future Outlook: StoneX plans to leverage the synergies from its recent acquisitions to enhance its market position and expand its service offerings across various financial markets. The company aims to continue focusing on strategic acquisitions and investments that align with its growth objectives, while maintaining a strong capital structure to support future opportunities.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Operational Risks: StoneX operates a global financial services network, exposing it to various market and operational risks. The business model relies on a diversified revenue stream across asset classes, client types, and geographies, which can be impacted by global economic conditions, regulatory changes, and technological advancements. The company aims to mitigate market risk and ensure liquidity through a centralized process for capital allocation and risk management.

Macroeconomic Risks: The company faces significant macroeconomic risks, including fluctuations in market conditions, interest rates, and trading volumes, which can adversely impact revenues and profitability. Geopolitical tensions, such as the war in Ukraine and the Israel-Hamas conflict, add to the uncertainty and potential volatility in commodity and financial markets.

Operational Risks: Reliance on third-party vendors for essential services, which, if disrupted, could affect client satisfaction and regulatory compliance. Cybersecurity threats are increasing in sophistication, posing risks to the company's trading platforms and client data.

Regulatory Risks: The complex and evolving global regulatory environment could lead to increased compliance costs and potential legal challenges. The company also faces competition risks from both large financial institutions and smaller, more agile competitors, which could impact market share and profitability.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by market volatility and regulatory changes. The company is focused on enhancing its risk management frameworks and investing in technology to improve operational resilience. Strategic initiatives include expanding into new markets and asset classes to diversify revenue streams and reduce dependency on any single market or client segment. The company is also pursuing acquisitions to strengthen its market position and capabilities.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and foreign currency exchange rate volatility, which could adversely affect financial results. The management employs hedging strategies to mitigate these risks but acknowledges that extreme market conditions could still lead to significant financial impacts. The company is also subject to credit risks from counterparties, which could result in financial losses if counterparties fail to meet their obligations.

SEC Filing: StoneX Group Inc. [ SNEX ] - 10-K - Nov. 28, 2025

data.title: T-REX Acquisition Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
T-REX Acquisition Corp., a company engaged in Bitcoin mining, hosting services, and software development, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities during the reporting period.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $9,258. The company experienced a decrease in revenue compared to the previous period, primarily due to lower mining revenue.

Gross Loss: $(52,670). The company reported a gross loss, indicating that the cost of goods sold exceeded total revenues.

Loss from Operations: $(605,458). Operating expenses increased significantly, driven by higher professional and management fees.

Net Loss: $(654,214). The net loss widened compared to the previous year, reflecting increased operating expenses and interest expenses.

Basic and Dilutive Net Loss Per Share: $(0.03). The loss per share increased, reflecting the higher net loss and an increase in the weighted average number of common shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

The company operates through four reportable segments: Holding, Mining, Hosting, and Software Services. The Mining segment generated revenue through Bitcoin mining activities, while the Hosting segment provided third-party hosting services at the Orofino facility. The Software Services segment is expected to generate future revenue from software development and licensing.

Geographical Performance

The company's operations and revenue generation were concentrated in Idaho, United States, during the reporting period. The Orofino facility in Idaho plays a significant role in the company's hosting and mining operations.

New Production Launches

In March 2025, the company acquired a data center and co-location facility in Orofino, Idaho, to support its cryptocurrency hosting business model. This facility offers third-party crypto miners support to operate mining operations without purchasing their own facilities.

Future Outlook

The company plans to continue focusing on securing and operating within the Bitcoin distributed ledger network and exploring additional distributed ledger protocols and infrastructure opportunities. Management intends to raise additional funds through public or private offerings to support operations and expand business activities.

SEC Filing: T-REX Acquisition Corp. [ TRXA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 28, 2025
